title: Chapter Sixty-Eight: The Miracle
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The banquet was held in the Sect Leader's hall courtyard, on the thirty-six marble steps that symbolized the thirty-six afflictions of the mundane world.

Yeon-shin had received the invitation at dawn. A summons from the Blood Flame Sect Leader himself, arranged at the petition of Gao Zong-xue and the Third Apostle.

*Yesterday, Gao Zong-xue followed me. He saw someone at the formation boundary. He reported it.*

This was a trial.

Ornate lanterns painted the steps in red and gold. Hundreds of evil sect warriors filled every level -- masters all, their fierce auras and madness-tinged boasting creating a chaos unique to the Blood Flame Sect. They spoke of beheadings and blood-draining the way farmers spoke of harvest.

"Welcome, Grand Teacher!" Gao Zong-xue stepped forward, courteous bearing wrapped around a faint sneer. "That you would accept our family's invitation! We are simply grateful."

Behind him, the reactions were less polished.

"Grand Teacher?"

"The Desolate Sect trash has come?"

"No clear advancement. Speaking too big."

Yeon-shin let the words pass through him. He scanned the hall. No allies here. But Desolate Sect's lord's presence had not left him since last night -- a warmth that wrapped his body like armor made of wind.

Gao Zong-xue guided him to the seat of honor. The man's satisfaction was poorly hidden.

*A place to bring down a lowborn. That is what this is.*

The banquet was a gallery of monsters. A scarred woman with both arms tattooed in blood-script boasted of draining a Wave Sect master's youngest son. Two brothers compared the quality of true qi they had absorbed from captured Shaolin monks -- "rich, but bland," one said, as though reviewing wine. A gaunt elder recounted razing a village and burning the remnants to stop the disease that always followed mass draining.

Yeon-shin walked among them and said nothing. He noted faces. Catalogued power levels. Counted exits.

The Blood Flame Sect Leader sat at the summit of the thirty-six steps. White hair cascading, red lips curved in an expression that could be amusement or cruelty or both. He raised a cup. His long fingers were elegant, deliberate.

"You are the pillars of our sect. The day approaches when you will display your extraordinary talents. We have taken in a Grand Teacher who will dye the sight of foolish ones black. He will modify blood techniques to wash away the world's hypocrisy. Raise a cup."

He drank. The masters drank after him. The wine was Du Kang -- the immortal wine of Henan, the drink Cao Cao had loved.

*The wine's meaning is strange. Ambition poured and served.*

"Gao Zong-xue," the Sect Leader said.

"Yes, Sect Leader."

Gao Zong-xue prostrated himself. Head down. His exposed back radiated triumph.

"Interrogate him."

"I receive the command."

The man rose and turned. His gaze found Yeon-shin.

*The matter is obvious.*

But there was no time for politics. The day the Sect Lord had given him was burning away with the sunset.

Gao Zong-xue cleared his throat, preparing some eloquent speech. Yeon-shin spoke first.

"In short. There is no progress in modifying the Blood Summoning Technique. Meanwhile, enemies brushed past the formation. A Desolate Sect person became Grand Teacher at a bad time. You wonder if I will cooperate."

Silence.

"The Grand Teacher's words are not wrong," Gao Zong-xue managed.

Yeon-shin raised his head toward the Sect Leader.

"I understand this gathering was held at the request of Gao Zong-xue and the Third Apostle. I am displeased. If I prove my usefulness in this place, I will take a treasure from the Gao family. If I cannot, you may behead me."

"Insolent," the Third Apostle muttered from his seat.

Yeon-shin paid no heed.

"I was going to ask about your progress anyway," the Sect Leader said. His smile was dizzying -- devilry wrapped in beauty. "I too was curious."

A bet with one's head on the line. The atmosphere shifted. Even evil sect masters enjoyed a good wager.

Yeon-shin gestured to a servant. A regular member -- red-haired, fanatical, eyes blank with devotion. The man approached without hesitation.

"Sit down and receive energy."

The servant sat. Closed his eyes. His bearing said he would not care if Yeon-shin killed him.

Yeon-shin placed his hand on the man's back.

He had spent a month studying the Blood Summoning Technique. A leisurely month -- more than enough time to modify a handful of verses. His blood technique true qi flowed through the servant's meridians, finding the paths that needed changing, altering them with the precision of a master calligrapher correcting a single stroke.

The reaction was immediate.

"Huh?"

"What is--?"

The servant's bright red hair began turning black. Not slowly, not strand by strand. Rapidly, visibly -- crimson draining to jet as if ink were being poured over his head.

The masters who had thrown insults at Yeon-shin fell silent. Empty gasps hung in the air.

Yeon-shin removed his hand.

The servant grabbed his now-black hair, trembling. "Grand Teacher... have I... for the first time, contained a miracle..."

But Yeon-shin was not looking at him. He was looking at Gao Zong-xue.

The man's face had gone pale blue. His hands, which had been clasped behind his back in affected leisure, hung limp at his sides.

Yeon-shin spoke quietly.

"The Poison Avoiding Pearl hanging on your neck."

*Whoosh!*

He raised the Demon Roaring Blood Technique deliberately. The blood-colored robe began to billow. Energy waves spread from him -- cold, bewitching, commanding.

"Present it to me. Now."

The Sect Leader's laughter broke from above like a dam bursting. Ecstatic. Maniacal. He swept his white hair aside and laughed and laughed, and the sound echoed off the marble steps.

Gao Zong-xue's hand went to his necklace. Trembling. Every member of the sect leadership watched in silence as the Third Apostle's son -- one of the top Blood Swordmasters in the Blood Flame Sect -- placed a jade pearl into Yeon-shin's palm.

The sensation was smooth against his fingers. Cool.

*Poison is more fearsome than any master in the world. And now I have its counter.*

"Grand Teacher!" The Sect Leader's voice boomed, warm and hearty -- a sound none of them had heard before. "Come here! I grant you my side!"

Empty gasps. Broken conventions. The verse modification had that much power.

Yeon-shin climbed the thirty-six steps and sat at the Blood Flame Sect Leader's right hand.

---
